hints.vultis and facite: There are three verb forms: 'vultis' (you wish), 'faciant' (they do — subjunctive), and 'facite' (do! — imperative)
hints.faciant only: 'faciant' is a subjunctive verb, but 'vultis' and 'facite' are also verb forms
hints.facite only: 'facite' is imperative, but 'vultis' (indicative) and 'faciant' (subjunctive) are also verbs

hints.to be strong: 'to be strong' is 'valere' — 'vultis' comes from 'velle' (to wish)

hints.perfect: Perfect 2nd plural would be 'voluistis' — 'vultis' is present
hints.future: Future of 'velle' is rare; 'vultis' is present indicative

hints.volo, volare, volavi, volatum: These are principal parts of 'volo' (to fly) — different verb!
hints.valeo, valere, valui, valitum: These are principal parts of 'valeo' (to be strong)
hints.verto, vertere, verti, versum: These are principal parts of 'verto' (to turn)

reveal.gloss: they should do / they may do

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'faciunt' — 'faciant' with -ant is subjunctive
hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'facite' (pl.) — 'faciant' is subjunctive in a purpose clause
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'facere' — 'faciant' is the present subjunctive

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are principal parts of 'fero' (to carry/bear)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are principal parts of 'fugio' (to flee)
hints.fallo, fallere, fefelli, falsum: These are principal parts of 'fallo' (to deceive)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd plural would be 'facitis' — 'facite' is imperative
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'faciatis' — 'facite' is the imperative plural
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'facere' — 'facite' is the imperative

hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are principal parts of 'dico' (to say)
hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are principal parts of 'ago' (to do/drive)
hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are principal parts of 'capio' (to seize)

translation: As you wish that men should do to you, do so to them
